what_it_does: Defines the catch-all 'prose' form: a non-empty string matcher that acts as the fallthrough route to the 'deep' band when no narrower form claims a unit.
observations: The module exports a FORM dict with id 'prose', a _match function, stage 'deep', policy 'capture_default', and fallthrough: True.
_match simply checks that the input is a non-empty string after stripping whitespace.
The docstring explains that route() evaluates narrow forms first and the fallthrough last, driven by the fallthrough flag rather than a hardcoded form name.
Fail-loud behavior is reserved for an empty registry or an all-False match set, not for normal units.
The file stem and form id are required to match ('prose').
conventions: Uses a module-level FORM dictionary to register the form.
Docstring describes behavior, ordering policy, and cross-file references without importing them.
Function name prefixed with underscore to indicate internal use.
notes: This is the default safety net: anything non-empty that no other form wants is treated as substantive prose and sent to deep processing.
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a simple, last-resort prose classifier that routes non-empty unmatched text to the deep stage.
apparent_themes: fail-loud for misconfiguration, not for missing matches
intention_signals: The design intends to avoid losing arbitrary text by giving it a default home in the deep band, while keeping the ordering generic via a fallthrough flag rather than special-casing this form.
novelty: The fallthrough flag abstracts ordering so the catch-all form does not need to be hardcoded as last; the same mechanism could reorder forms generically.
contribution: Provides a minimal, reusable non-empty-string matcher and a default deep-stage form that prevents unclaimed units from being dropped.
summary_for_embedding: company/forms/prose.py defines the 'prose' fallthrough form that classifies any non-empty string as free prose and routes it to the deep stage.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to serve as the catch-all default classifier so that unmatched textual units are treated as substantive prose rather than rejected.

symbols.does: Returns True if the input is a non-empty string after stripping whitespace.
symbols.description: A private predicate used by the 'prose' form to decide whether a unit qualifies as free prose; it is intentionally permissive so the form can act as a catch-all for any non-empty text.
